calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidence And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Amazon.Com, Inc.
Off Calendar Mar-07-2023 Off Calendar

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT. (PER EX PARTE ORDER FILED ON 3-1-23)
Off Calendar Mar-07-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT. (PER EX PARTE ORDER FILED ON 3-1-23)

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Cliff Chanler, Esq. of CHANLER LLC, Tel:203.594.9246, for plaintiff/moving party;
Brett Oberst, Esq. of DOLL AMIR & ELEY LLP, Tel:213.542.3380, for defendant.

HEARING HELD.

Mr. Chanler explained why no courtesy copies of the motions in CGC-21-595147 Vinocur v. Amazon and CGC-22-598060 Vinocur v. Amazon were submitted to the court.

Mr. Chanler also stated that he does not need a ruling on the 2 other motions.

Therefore:

1) Plaintiff's Motion for Entry of Consent Judgment in CGC-21-595147 Vinocur v. Amazon and

2) Plaintiff's Motion for Entry of Consent Judgment in CGC-22-598060 Vinocur v. Amazon are

ordered OFF CALENDAR by the court.

In addition, one of the provisions of the consent judgment is for the court to consolidate the 3 cases under the case CGC-21-594849 Wozniak v. Amazon.

Mr. Oberst does not oppose the consent judgment.

After hearing, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT is granted.

Order and judgment were signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
